findings.title: provision_interpreter's core orchestration (extract zip, patch _pth, bootstrap pip, install pinned deps + backlink_publisher, remove build-tools path, pip-audit, atomic move) has no fast/mocked happy-path test
findings.suggested_fix: Add a mocked-through test (monkeypatching _http_get_to_file for both the embed zip and get-pip.py, subprocess.run for _bootstrap_pip/_run_embedded_pip/_run_pip_audit, and providing a fake extracted zip fixture with python.exe + a real ._pth file) that drives provision_interpreter end-to-end and asserts: (a) the pinned-requirements file is written and passed via -r to the pip install step, (b) _remove_build_tools_path runs before _run_pip_audit (order matters per the docstring), (c) the staging dir is only moved to output_dir after every step succeeds. Currently only the two early pre-flight-abort paths (checksum mismatch, unknown version) are unit tested (test_build_windows_package.py lines 81-137); the entire success path is exercised only by the real_packaging_build-gated e2e test, which never runs in CI.

findings.title: _bootstrap_pip and _run_embedded_pip non-zero-exit error paths have zero test coverage, unlike sibling subprocess wrappers (_run_pip_audit, _run_npm_step) which are explicitly tested
findings.suggested_fix: Add tests mirroring test_run_pip_audit_aborts_on_known_vulnerabilities / test_build_spa_raises_on_npm_ci_failure: monkeypatch bwp.subprocess.run to return a non-zero CompletedProcess and assert _bootstrap_pip / _run_embedded_pip raise BuildError with the step_label and stdout/stderr embedded in the message.

findings.title: _create_zip_and_checksum's error paths (archive-creation exception cleanup, and the defensive make_archive path-mismatch check) are entirely untested, and build_package's separate 'BUILD FAILED at zip/checksum step' except block is never triggered by any test
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test that monkeypatches shutil.make_archive to raise, calling _create_zip_and_checksum (or build_package with all six stages mocked) directly, and assert BuildError is raised with the partial-zip-cleanup guarantee (zip_path.unlink() called / no stale zip left) and that build_package's zip-step except block prints 'BUILD FAILED at zip/checksum step' and re-raises without producing a .zip. The only current zip/checksum coverage is the happy path via test_build_package_happy_path_produces_zip_and_checksum and the gated real e2e test.

findings.title: build_package's stage-failure parametrized test omits copy_helper_scripts/copy_docs, and the generic 'except Exception as exc' catch-all (non-BuildError -> BuildError conversion) is never exercised by any test
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the fail_at parametrize list in test_build_package_stage_failure_propagates_and_produces_no_zip to include 'copy_helper_scripts' and 'copy_docs' for symmetry, and add one test where a mocked stage raises a plain RuntimeError (not bwp.BuildError) to prove the generic except-Exception branch correctly wraps it as BuildError('Unexpected error during build: ...') and still leaves no zip.

findings.title: main()'s --version CLI argument is never exercised through argparse -- only build_package(version=...) is tested directly with a version kwarg
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test calling bwp.main(['--version', '2.5.0']) with build_package mocked, asserting build_package was invoked with version='2.5.0' (proves the argparse-to-orchestration wiring, not just build_package's own logic).

residual_risks: The provisioning step that actually installs packages into the shipped interpreter (_bootstrap_pip, both _run_embedded_pip calls inside provision_interpreter, and their sequencing relative to _assert_catalog_resources/_remove_build_tools_path/_run_pip_audit) is verified for correctness only by the opt-in real_packaging_build e2e test (Windows + network + BACKLINK_PUBLISHER_REAL_PACKAGING_BUILD=1), which never runs in CI. A regression that breaks the install-step wiring, argument order, or error-message formatting in this section would ship silently through normal CI runs and only surface the next time an operator manually runs the real build.

Unlike Unit 1 (provision_interpreter), Units 2 (generate_cli_shims) and 3 (assemble_package) both have full mocked/lightweight happy-path coverage in the fast tier that closely parallels what their real gated tests exercise, so the overall gating strategy is reasonable for those units but asymmetric for Unit 1.
testing_gaps: provision_interpreter (scripts/packaging/build_windows_package.py:949-1062) has no fast/mocked test exercising its full success path -- only the checksum-mismatch and unknown-version pre-flight-abort branches are unit tested; the multi-step pip-install/patch/audit/move orchestration is proven only by the network+Windows-gated real test.
_bootstrap_pip (line 279) and _run_embedded_pip (line 296) have no test at all, mocked or real-isolated, for their non-zero-exit BuildError-wrapping behavior.
_create_zip_and_checksum's exception-cleanup and defensive-check branches (lines 1165-1213) and build_package's corresponding zip-step except block (lines 1290-1299) have zero test coverage.

The gating strategy for @pytest.mark.real_packaging_build tests (opt-in via BACKLINK_PUBLISHER_REAL_PACKAGING_BUILD=1 + win32 skipif, network unblocked via pytest_socket.enable_socket()) mirrors the existing real_live_publish pattern in this repo and is reasonable overall; the fast/mocked tier gives strong parity with the real tests for Units 2 (CLI shim generation) and 3 (SPA/package assembly), but weak parity for Unit 1 (interpreter provisioning) as detailed above.
